sources.issuing_organisation: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee for Diabetes
sources.exact_document_title: 4. Comprehensive Medical Evaluation and Assessment of Comorbidities: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026
sources.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation
sources.population: People with diabetes, subject to age, diabetes type, pregnancy, complication and comorbidity qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial, follow-up and annual comprehensive diabetes evaluation.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected history, treatment, glycaemic-status, complication, comorbidity, function, support, risk-factor, shared-goal and continuing-care documentation. It does not generate a diagnosis or management plan.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 4.3–4.4 — comprehensive evaluation and ongoing management
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting glycaemic status, current treatment, complications, comorbidities, overall and functional status, support, risk factors, shared goals and continuing-care plans.

exact_sections.heading: Components of the comprehensive diabetes medical evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded initial, follow-up and annual medical history, medicines, hypoglycaemia, monitoring, blood pressure, weight, examination, laboratory and complication-screening context.

archive.title: 4. Comprehensive Medical Evaluation and Assessment of Comorbidities: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026
archive.publisher: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee for Diabetes
archive.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation
archive.population: People with diabetes, subject to age, diabetes type, pregnancy, complication and comorbidity qualifiers.
archive.setting: Initial, follow-up and annual comprehensive diabetes evaluation.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 4.3–4.4 — comprehensive evaluation and ongoing management
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting glycaemic status, current treatment, complications, comorbidities, overall and functional status, support, risk factors, shared goals and continuing-care plans.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Components of the comprehensive diabetes medical evaluation
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded initial, follow-up and annual medical history, medicines, hypoglycaemia, monitoring, blood pressure, weight, examination, laboratory and complication-screening context.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30
sources.population: Adults with type 2 diabetes; specific diagnostic exclusions are stated for children, pregnancy, and selected conditions.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult Dubai telehealth type 2 diabetes review; partial because the baseline workflow has no explicit adult-only filter and is not limited to telehealth.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth type 2 diabetes scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5.6 Annual/6 monthly review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Referral Criteria and Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

exact_sections.heading: 7.2 Refilling/Prescribing Diabetic Medication — monitoring prerequisites
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 7 — summary of required laboratory tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.
